text: Find $|\mathbf{a} \times \mathbf{b}|^2$, if $|\mathbf{a}| = 2$, $|\mathbf{b}| = 3$ and angle between $\mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbf{b}$ is $\dfrac{\pi}{6}$
name: Question Item 1

text: If $\mathbf{a} = \alpha \hat{\mathbf{i}} + 2\hat{\mathbf{j}} - 6\hat{\mathbf{k}}$ and $\mathbf{b} = 2\hat{\mathbf{i}} - \hat{\mathbf{j}} + \beta \hat{\mathbf{k}}$, then the values of $\alpha, \beta$ so that $\mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbf{b}$ may be collinear are
name: Question Item 2

text:
Let $\mathbf{a}, \mathbf{b}, \mathbf{c}$ be three vectors
(i) $(\mathbf{a} \times \mathbf{b}) \times \mathbf{c} = (\mathbf{a} \cdot \mathbf{c})\mathbf{b} - (\mathbf{b} \cdot \mathbf{c})\mathbf{a}$
(ii) $\mathbf{a} \times (\mathbf{b} \times \mathbf{c}) = (\mathbf{a} \cdot \mathbf{b})\mathbf{c} - (\mathbf{a} \cdot \mathbf{c})\mathbf{b}$
The correct statement is
explanation: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect.
name: Question Item 3

text:
ABC is a right-angled triangle in which $\max\{AB, BC, AC\} = BC$. If the position vectors of B and C are respectively $3\hat{\mathbf{i}} - 2\hat{\mathbf{j}} + \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ and $5\hat{\mathbf{i}} + \hat{\mathbf{j}} - 3\hat{\mathbf{k}}$, then
$AB \cdot AC + BA \cdot BC + CA \cdot CB =$
explanation:
$\overrightarrow{BC} = 2\hat{\mathbf{i}} + 3\hat{\mathbf{j}} - 4\hat{\mathbf{k}}$
$BC$ is hypotenuse $\implies \angle A = 90^\circ$
$AB \cdot AC = 0$
$AB \cdot BC + BA \cdot BC + CA \cdot CB = |BC|^2 = 4 + 9 + 16 = 29$.
name: Question Item 4

text: If $\mathbf{a} + \mathbf{b} + \mathbf{c} = \mathbf{0}$ and $|\mathbf{a}| = 3$, $|\mathbf{b}| = 5$, $|\mathbf{c}| = 7$, then the angle between $\mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbf{b}$ is
name: Question Item 5

text: If $\mathbf{a}, \mathbf{b}, \mathbf{c}$ and $\mathbf{r}$ are vectors such that $\mathbf{a}$ is not perpendicular to $\mathbf{b}$, $\mathbf{r} \times \mathbf{b} = \mathbf{c} \times \mathbf{b}$ and $\mathbf{r} \cdot \mathbf{a} = 0$ then $\mathbf{r} =$
name: Question Item 6

text:
In $\triangle ABC$ state whether the following are true or false
(i) $\overrightarrow{AB} + \overrightarrow{BC} + \overrightarrow{CA} = \mathbf{0}$
(ii) $\overrightarrow{AB} + \overrightarrow{BC} - \overrightarrow{AC} = \mathbf{0}$
(iii) $\overrightarrow{AB} - \overrightarrow{CB} + \overrightarrow{CA} = \mathbf{0}$
(iv) $\overrightarrow{AB} + \overrightarrow{BC} - \overrightarrow{CA} = \mathbf{0}$
name: Question Item 7

text: If the vectors $\alpha \hat{\mathbf{i}} + \hat{\mathbf{j}} + \hat{\mathbf{k}}$, $\hat{\mathbf{i}} + \beta \hat{\mathbf{j}} + \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ and $\hat{\mathbf{i}} + \hat{\mathbf{j}} + \gamma \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ are coplanar, where $(\alpha, \beta, \gamma \neq 1)$, then the value of $\dfrac{1}{1-\alpha} + \dfrac{1}{1-\beta} + \dfrac{1}{1-\gamma} =$
explanation:
For coplanar vectors the determinant is zero
$\begin{vmatrix} \alpha & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & \beta & 1 \\ 1 & 1 & \gamma \end{vmatrix} = 0$
After simplification $\dfrac{1}{1-\alpha} + \dfrac{1}{1-\beta} + \dfrac{1}{1-\gamma} = 1$.
name: Question Item 8

text: Let $\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}$ and $\mathbf{w}$ be three vectors in $\mathbb{R}^3$. Then, any vector $\mathbf{z} \in \mathbb{R}^3$ can be written as $\mathbf{z} = a\mathbf{u} + b\mathbf{v} + c\mathbf{w}$ for some scalars $a, b$ and $c$ if and only if
options: Each pair of $\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}$ and $\mathbf{w}$ are not parallel
Each of $\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}$ and $\mathbf{w}$ can be written as a linear combination of the other two
All have different magnitude and directions
None of the options are correct
explanation: If $\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{w}$ are linearly dependent (not forming basis), any vector cannot be expressed uniquely. Hence none of the options.
name: Question Item 9

text: If $\overrightarrow{PO} + \overrightarrow{OQ} = \overrightarrow{QO} + \overrightarrow{OR}$, then
explanation: From vector equality $\overrightarrow{QP} = \overrightarrow{QR}$ implies $|QP| = |QR|$, so Q is mid-point of PR.
name: Question Item 10

text: Let $\mathbf{u}$ and $\mathbf{v}$ be two non-zero vectors. Then the magnitude of the cross product $\mathbf{u} \times \mathbf{v}$ is always
name: Question Item 11

name: Question Item 12

name: Question Item 13

text: If $\alpha$ is the angle between two vectors $\mathbf{p} = 3\hat{\mathbf{i}} + 4\hat{\mathbf{j}} - \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ and $\mathbf{q} = 2\hat{\mathbf{i}} - \hat{\mathbf{j}} + \hat{\mathbf{k}}$, then $\sin(\alpha) =$
name: Question Item 14

text: Equation of the perpendicular bisector of the line joining the points whose position vectors are $\mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbf{b}$ respectively is
name: Question Item 15

text: If $\mathbf{a} = 2\hat{\mathbf{i}} + \hat{\mathbf{j}} - 3\hat{\mathbf{k}}$ and $\mathbf{b} = 3\hat{\mathbf{i}} - \hat{\mathbf{j}} + 2\hat{\mathbf{k}}$, then find the angle between the vectors $2\mathbf{a} + \mathbf{b}$ and $\mathbf{a} + 2\mathbf{b}$
name: Question Item 16

text: If the magnitude of the vector product of the vector $\hat{\mathbf{i}} + \hat{\mathbf{j}} + \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ with a unit vector along the sum of the vectors $2\hat{\mathbf{i}} + 4\hat{\mathbf{j}} - 5\hat{\mathbf{k}}$ and $\lambda \hat{\mathbf{i}} + 2\hat{\mathbf{j}} + 3\hat{\mathbf{k}}$ is equal to $\sqrt{2}$, then the value of $\lambda$ is
explanation:
Unit vector along sum $\dfrac{(2+\lambda)\hat{\mathbf{i}} + 6\hat{\mathbf{j}} - 2\hat{\mathbf{k}}}{\sqrt{\lambda^2 + 40}}$
Magnitude condition yields $\lambda = 1$.
name: Question Item 17

text:
Find $\lambda$ if $\mathbf{a}, \mathbf{b}, \mathbf{c}$ are three non-coplanar vectors such that
$[4\mathbf{a} + 3\mathbf{b} - \mathbf{c} \quad 4\mathbf{a} + 3\mathbf{b} + 2\mathbf{c} \quad \mathbf{a} - 4\mathbf{b} - \mathbf{c}] = (\lambda^2 + \lambda + 1)[\mathbf{a} \quad \mathbf{b} \quad \mathbf{c}]$
name: Question Item 18

text: In a regular hexagon $ABCDEF$, $\overrightarrow{AD} + \overrightarrow{EB} + \overrightarrow{FC} = (3\lambda - 8)\overrightarrow{AB}$. Then $\lambda =$
name: Question Item 19

text: Two vectors $\mathbf{u}$ and $\mathbf{v}$ are parallel if and only if
options: One of them is a scalar multiple of the other
The dot product of $\mathbf{u}$ and $\mathbf{v}$ is zero
explanation: Parallel vectors: one is scalar multiple of other.
name: Question Item 20

text: Let $\mathbf{u}$ and $\mathbf{v}$ be two non-zero vectors. If $|\mathbf{u} + \mathbf{v}| = |\mathbf{u} - \mathbf{v}|$, then
name: Question Item 21

text: The unit vectors orthogonal to $3\hat{\mathbf{i}} + 2\hat{\mathbf{j}} + 6\hat{\mathbf{k}}$ and coplanar with $2\hat{\mathbf{i}} + \hat{\mathbf{j}}$ and $\hat{\mathbf{i}} - \hat{\mathbf{j}} + \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ are
explanation:
Coplanar vector: $a(2\hat{\mathbf{i}} + \hat{\mathbf{j}}) + b(\hat{\mathbf{i}} - \hat{\mathbf{j}} + \hat{\mathbf{k}})$
Orthogonal to given: yields $\pm \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{10}} (3\hat{\mathbf{j}} - \hat{\mathbf{k}})$.
name: Question Item 22

text: If $\mathbf{a} = 2\hat{\mathbf{i}} - 3\hat{\mathbf{j}} - 4\hat{\mathbf{k}}$, $\mathbf{b} = \hat{\mathbf{i}} + 4\hat{\mathbf{j}} - 2\hat{\mathbf{k}}$, $\mathbf{c} = 3\hat{\mathbf{i}} - \hat{\mathbf{j}} + 4\hat{\mathbf{k}}$ then, $[\mathbf{a} \times \mathbf{b} \quad \mathbf{b} \times \mathbf{c} \quad \mathbf{c} \times \mathbf{a}] =$
name: Question Item 23

text: Let $\mathbf{u}$ and $\mathbf{v}$ be two vectors in a plane. Then any vector $\mathbf{w}$ in the plane can be written as $\mathbf{w} = a\mathbf{u} + b\mathbf{v}$ for some scalars 'a' and 'b' if and only if
options: None of $\mathbf{u}$ and $\mathbf{v}$ is a scalar multiple of the other
None of $|\mathbf{u}|$ and $|\mathbf{v}|$ is a scalar multiple of the other
$\mathbf{u}$ and $\mathbf{v}$ are perpendicular to each other
name: Question Item 24

name: Question Item 25

text: Let $\mathbf{u} = \hat{\mathbf{i}} - 2\hat{\mathbf{j}}$ and $\mathbf{v} = -3\hat{\mathbf{i}} + 5\hat{\mathbf{j}}$. Consider three points $P$, $Q$ and $R$ having position vectors $-\dfrac{1}{7}\hat{\mathbf{i}}$, $-\dfrac{1}{4}\hat{\mathbf{j}}$ and $-2\hat{\mathbf{i}} + 3\hat{\mathbf{j}}$ respectively. Among these, the points in the line segment passing through $\mathbf{u}$ and $\mathbf{v}$ are
explanation: Line equation satisfies P and Q, not R.
name: Question Item 27

explanation: Using section formula and vector addition: $5\overrightarrow{AN} = 3\overrightarrow{AC}$.
